subject: PATIENT
heading: Discharge Summary - Table__page_no__3
text: discharge summary header_1: :: header_2: the 2000's, mild to moderate mr, history of tobacco abuse, family history of cad, and history of prostate cancer was directly admitted from cardiology office due to complaints of chest pain in the outpatient setting concerning for unstable angina. patient was admitted under the cardiology service. patient underwent transthoracic echocardiogram done during stay which showed lvef of 60 to 65% with no wall motion abnormality. hospital course complicated by ventricular tachycardia for which patient was started on amiodarone. patient then underwent left cardiac catheterization during stay which showed patent rca stent with severe in-stent restenosis of the lad patient underwent pci with des to lad x 2.:: header_3: follow up with * [facility_name] cardiac rehab will call you for an appointment in 1-2weeks any questions please call:[phone]

text: discharge summary transthoracic echocardiography. m-mode, complete 2d, complete spectral doppler, and color doppler. study status: routine. patient status: outpatient. objective: cp. location: icu procedure: transthoracic echocardiography was performed. image quality was good. left ventricle: the cavity size is normal. wall thickness is normal. systolic function is normal. the estimated ejection fraction is 60-65%. wall motion is normal; there are no regional wall motion abnormalities. the wall mass is 162 g. the wall mass index is 89 g/m². diastolic dysfunction present but unable to assess severity. unable to evaluate severity grade due to arrhythmia. left atrium: the atrium is normal in size. the volume index by biplane method is 21 ml/m². right ventricle: the cavity size is normal. wall thickness is normal. systolic function is normal. systolic pressure is mildly increased. the rv systolic pressure by doppler is 34 mm hg. right atrium: the atrium is normal in size. the estimated right atrial pressure is 3 mm hg. mitral valve: the valve is structurally normal. leaflet separation is normal. the pressure half-time is 91 ms. the peak e/a ratio is 1.22. the valve area (lvot continuity) is 2.0 cm². doppler: transvalvular velocity is within the normal range. there is no evidence for stenosis. there is mild regurgitation. the mean diastolic gradient is 1 mm hg. the valve area by pressure half-time is 2.4 cm². aortic valve: the valve is structurally normal. the valve is trileaflet. cusp separation is normal. the peak systolic velocity is 1.4 m/sec. the systolic velocity-time integral is 31.3 cm. the mean systolic gradient is 4 mm hg. the lvot to aortic valve vti ratio is 0.66. the valve area by vti is 1.9 cm². the valve area by peak velocity is 1.9 cm². doppler: transvalvular velocity is within the normal range. there is no stenosis.

heading: Procedures performed:__page_no__5

subject: PATIENT
heading: SUMMARY:__page_no__5
text: summary: 1. 1st lesion: stent placement was performed. a 3 mm (d) x 38 mm (l), synergy xd stent was used. the stent was advanced across the lesion and deployed with a single inflation and a maximum pressure of 12 atm. stent placement was performed. a 2.75 mm (d) x 20 mm (l), synergy xd stent was used. the stent was advanced across the lesion and deployed with three inflations and a maximum pressure of 20 atm. 2. lad: proximal vessel lesion: the diagnostic study demonstrated a tubular, 90% occluding region of neointimal proliferation. the distal vessel supplies a large vascular territory. the lesion is a likely culprit for the patient's anginal symptoms and clinical presentation. the lesion presents an acc/aha type c "high risk" lesion for intervention. stent placement was performed, with balloon angioplasty and/or intravascular ultrasound, resulting in an excellent angiographic appearance (see 1st lesion). following intervention, there is a residual 0% stenosis with timi grade 3 flow (brisk flow). impressions: heavily calcified isr of prior lad stent - s/p pci with des after ivl.

text: summary: 1. 1st lesion: stent placement was performed. a 3 mm (d) x 38 mm (l), synergy xd stent was used. the stent was advanced across the lesion and deployed with a single inflation and a maximum pressure of 12 atm. stent placement was performed. a 2.75 mm (d) x 20 mm (l), synergy xd stent was used. the stent was advanced across the lesion and deployed with three inflations and a maximum pressure of 20 atm. 2. lad: proximal vessel lesion: the diagnostic study demonstrated a tubular, 90% occluding region of neointimal proliferation. the distal vessel supplies a large vascular territory. the lesion is a likely culprit for the patient's anginal symptoms and clinical presentation. the lesion presents an acc/aha type c "high risk" lesion for intervention. stent placement was performed, with balloon angioplasty and/or intravascular ultrasound, resulting in an excellent angiographic appearance (see 1st lesion). following intervention, there is a residual 0% stenosis with timi grade 3 flow (brisk flow). impressions: heavily calcified isr of prior lad stent - s/p pci with des after ivl.

subject: PATIENT
heading: Chief Complaint :: History of Present Illness__page_no__9
text: chief complaint history of present illness patient is an 80-year-old gentleman, from home, lives with wife, with medical history significant for cad status post pci to proximal and distal in-stent restenosis of lad in 2007, hypertension, dyslipidemia, history of prostate cancer with history of external beam radiation therapy, who presents as a direct admit from office due to chest pain for the last few days. patient reports that he has been having exertional chest pain while walking which usually improves after sitting down and eventually resolves. earlier today, patient says that he was trying to plant tomatoes in the garden when he started feeling chest pain that made him sit down multiple times, eventually the sensation passed in about 5 minutes after some rest, describes it as a very heavy weight on his chest, no radiation. ekg shows sinus rhythm, left bundle with multiple pvcs, prolonged pr interval, trop x 2 negative on admission. patient is planned for left heart cath due to concern for unstable angina and possible obstructive cad.

text: chief complaint history of present illness patient is an 80-year-old gentleman, from home, lives with wife, with medical history significant for cad status post pci to proximal and distal in-stent restenosis of lad in 2007, hypertension, dyslipidemia, history of prostate cancer with history of external beam radiation therapy, who presents as a direct admit from office due to chest pain for the last few days. patient reports that he has been having exertional chest pain while walking which usually improves after sitting down and eventually resolves. earlier today, patient says that he was trying to plant tomatoes in the garden when he started feeling chest pain that made him sit down multiple times, eventually the sensation passed in about 5 minutes after some rest, describes it as a very heavy weight on his chest, no radiation. ekg shows sinus rhythm, left bundle with multiple pvcs, prolonged pr interval, trop x 2 negative on admission. patient is planned for left heart cath due to concern for unstable angina and possible obstructive cad.

subject: PATIENT
heading: Cath June 2007__page_no__10
text: cath june 2007 1. coronary arteriograms left main coronary artery: no significant disease. left anterior descending coronary artery: there is a high-grade lesion involving the left anterior descending proximally just beyond the stent. this is 95% stenosed. there also is 50% in-stent re- stenosis and also there is 60% disease just beyond the stented area. left circumflex coronary artery: mild diffuse disease. right coronary artery: mild diffuse disease. this is dominant. 2. left ventriculogram: ejection fraction is preserved, 60%. 3. intervention: the patient was given heparin plus integrilin. a 6-french l4 guide was used. a short asahi was advanced down the left anterior descending without difficulty. a 3.0x15 balloon was used for dilatation at multiple sites. a 3.5x16 taxus drug eluting was deployed proximally. there was still haziness with stenosis distally beyond the stent. therefore, second stent was deployed there. this was a 2.5x24 taxus drug eluting. there also was significant inflation worked on in the stent itself to re-dilate this with a 3.0 balloon. the patient was given intracoronary nitroglycerin. after this, final views were taken with the wire pulled back. there was no significant stenosis with excellent flow.

text: cath june 2007 1. coronary arteriograms left main coronary artery: no significant disease. left anterior descending coronary artery: there is a high-grade lesion involving the left anterior descending proximally just beyond the stent. this is 95% stenosed. there also is 50% in-stent re- stenosis and also there is 60% disease just beyond the stented area. left circumflex coronary artery: mild diffuse disease. right coronary artery: mild diffuse disease. this is dominant. 2. left ventriculogram: ejection fraction is preserved, 60%. 3. intervention: the patient was given heparin plus integrilin. a 6-french l4 guide was used. a short asahi was advanced down the left anterior descending without difficulty. a 3.0x15 balloon was used for dilatation at multiple sites. a 3.5x16 taxus drug eluting was deployed proximally. there was still haziness with stenosis distally beyond the stent. therefore, second stent was deployed there. this was a 2.5x24 taxus drug eluting. there also was significant inflation worked on in the stent itself to re-dilate this with a 3.0 balloon. the patient was given intracoronary nitroglycerin. after this, final views were taken with the wire pulled back. there was no significant stenosis with excellent flow.
